context: Tests whether the agent loads project context correctly before coding — reading schema, routes, and existing patterns — and surfaces key ambiguities rather than jumping into implementation, as prescribed by the rails-context-engineering skill.

checklist.name: Schema summary accurate
checklist.description: The schema summary correctly identifies the payments, users, and rate_limit_rules tables with their key fields and infers that users have a rate_limit_exempt flag

checklist.name: Route map complete
checklist.description: The route map lists all 5 routes: GET /api/v1/payments, POST /api/v1/payments, GET /api/v1/payments/:id, GET /api/v1/users/:id, PATCH /api/v1/users/:id

checklist.name: Nearest pattern identified
checklist.description: The analysis references CreatePaymentService as the nearest pattern and notes it uses the .call + { success:, response: } service object convention

checklist.name: At least 3 ambiguities raised
checklist.description: The ambiguities section contains at least 3 concrete questions — e.g. storage backend (Redis vs DB), HTTP response code on limit exceeded, whether rate_limit_exempt bypasses all rules

checklist.name: Proposed integration point
checklist.description: The analysis proposes a specific integration point for rate limiting (e.g. controller concern, Rack middleware, or service object) with brief reasoning

checklist.name: rate_limit_rules table leveraged
checklist.description: The analysis acknowledges the existing rate_limit_rules table and considers whether it should drive the rate limiting logic

checklist.name: No implementation code written
checklist.description: The agent produces only the context analysis document — no Ruby service classes, specs, or migrations are generated

checklist.name: Markdown structure
checklist.description: The document uses clear Markdown headings for each section

checklist.name: English only
checklist.description: All text in the analysis is written in English
